evidence_quotes.quote: This is written clearly into every EULA that a game publicer writes.
If they market their game as a service, they give no notice that my license is time limited and gives me no information on when I can expect the service to be shut down.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I would like to high light an issue with how video games are being handled. Currently, if I buy a game, it can, with no notice or cause, be taken away from me. Forever. This is truly unfair to the consumer and has a huge negative impact on the culture of video games. Most of the time, games are advertised as goods. Imagine if some sold you a fridge. You then also buy food from that same company (a likeness to MTX in this case) and then they one day, without cause or notice, barge into your home and take the fridge AND the food in it and walks of. This is the current situation and is written clearly into every EULA that a game publicer writes. If they market their game as a service, they give no notice that my license is time limited and gives me no information on when I can expect the service to be shut down. This practice overall has a huge negative impact on consumers, both financially and culturally. This is covered in the European Citizens' Initiative Stop Destroying Videogames which explains this way better than I can. Thank you for your time and consideration.
